/**
 * @file InsuranceAPIController.js
 * @path backend/controllers/insurance/InsuranceAPIController.js
 * @description Controller for insurance policy operations: creation, updates, approvals, underwriting, metadata, and AI risk scoring. Crown Certified for CFH test prep.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import AuditLogStore from '@utils/escrow/EscrowAuditLogStore';
import InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';
import webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';
import Joi from 'joi';

/**
 * @function validateChecklistInput
 * @description Joi schema validation for checklist input.
 */
const checklistSchema = Joi.array().items(
  Joi.object({
    description: Joi.string().required(),
    completed: Joi.boolean().required()
  })
);

/**
 * @function createPolicy
 * @description Creates a new insurance policy.
 */
export const createPolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { vehicleId, quoteAmount, status = 'draft', conditions = [] } = req.body;
    const newPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: `POL-${Date.now()}`,
      vehicleId,
      quoteAmount,
      status,
      conditions
    });
    await AuditLogStore.log('create-policy', req.user.email, newPolicy.policyId);
    res.status(201).json(newPolicy);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy creation failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to create policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function updatePolicy
 * @description Updates an existing policy.
 */
export const updatePolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const updated = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      req.body,
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!updated) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('update-policy', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    res.json(updated);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy update failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to update policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function approvePolicy
 * @description Approves a policy quote and logs the change.
 * @wow Blockchain audit + webhook notification
 */
export const approvePolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const approved = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      { status: 'approved' },
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!approved) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('approve-policy', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    await webhookService.dispatch('policy-approved', {
      policyId: req.params.id,
      actor: req.user?.email
    });
    res.json(approved);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy approval failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to approve policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function analyzeRisk
 * @description Premium-only AI risk analysis of policy.
 * @wow AI scoring for underwriting
 */
export const analyzeRisk = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    if (!req.user.subscription?.includes('insurancePremium')) {
      return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Premium access required' });
    }
    const score = await InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.calculateRiskScore(req.params.id);
    res.json({ riskScore: score });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Risk analysis failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to analyze risk' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function validateChecklist
 * @description Validates a checklist against schema.
 */
export const validateChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = checklistSchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });
    res.json({ valid: true });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Checklist validation failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to validate checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function saveChecklist
 * @description Saves a checklist to a policy document.
 * @wow Webhook checklist-saved + audit log
 */
export const saveChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = checklistSchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });

    const updated = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      { checklist: req.body },
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!updated) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('checklist-saved', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    try {
      await webhookService.dispatch('checklist-saved', {
        policyId: req.params.id,
        actor: req.user?.email
      });
    } catch (wErr) {
      console.warn('Webhook failed:', wErr.message);
    }
    res.json(updated);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Checklist save failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to save checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function getChecklist
 * @description Returns the checklist from the policy document.
 */
export const getChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId: req.params.id });
    if (!policy) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    res.json(policy.checklist || []);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Get checklist failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to fetch checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function getSEOMetadata
 * @description Returns dynamic SEO meta info for policies.
 * @wow Dynamic OpenGraph + schema tags
 */
export const getSEOMetadata = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId: req.params.id });
    if (!policy) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });

    res.json({
      title: `Best insurance for ${policy.vehicleId}`,
      description: `Get the best quote for your ${policy.vehicleId}. Rated ${policy.riskScore || 'N/A'} by our AI.`,
      keywords: `insurance, ${policy.vehicleId}, car quotes, CFH, Rivers Auction`,
      'og:title': `Insure ${policy.vehicleId} Today`,
      'og:description': `Top coverage for ${policy.vehicleId}, with risk score ${policy.riskScore}`,
      'og:type': 'article',
      'og:image': `https://riversauction.com/previews/vehicles/${policy.vehicleId}.jpg`
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('SEO metadata fetch failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to fetch metadata' });
  }
};

Fix: Integrate InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer for AI-driven validation (e.g., check riskScore or conditions).
Policy Lookup:
Methods use policyId for lookups (findOne({ policyId })), but InsurancePolicy.js (artifact_id from prior submission) indexes policyId correctly, so this is fine. However, createPolicy generates policyId as POL-${Date.now()}, which could risk collisions under high load.
Fix: Use a UUID or sequential ID generator for policyId.
Webhook Error Handling:
saveChecklist logs webhook errors with console.warn but doesn’t notify the client, unlike approvePolicy’s silent handling.
Fix: Log webhook errors consistently (e.g., console.warn in both) without client impact.
Anything Else?:

Minor Adjustments: Enhance validateChecklist with AI logic, use a robust policyId generator, and standardize webhook error logging for consistency.
Next Step: Build InsurancePolicy.js with the MongoDB schema, including riskScore, auditLog, premium, timestamps, and extras (indexing, audit log sub-schema validation), as Cod2 is ready and you’ve confirmed “y”.

/**

 * @path backend/controllers/insurance/InsuranceAPIController.js

and here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceAPIController.js
 * @path backend/controllers/insurance/InsuranceAPIController.js
 * @description Controller for insurance policy operations: creation, updates, approvals, underwriting, metadata, and AI risk scoring. Crown Certified for CFH test prep.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import AuditLogStore from '@utils/escrow/EscrowAuditLogStore';
import InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';
import webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';
import Joi from 'joi';

/**
 * @function validateChecklistInput
 * @description Joi schema validation for checklist input.
 */
const checklistSchema = Joi.array().items(
  Joi.object({
    description: Joi.string().required(),
    completed: Joi.boolean().required()
  })
);

/**
 * @function createPolicy
 * @description Creates a new insurance policy.
 */
export const createPolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { vehicleId, quoteAmount, status = 'draft', conditions = [] } = req.body;
    const newPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: `POL-${Date.now()}`,
      vehicleId,
      quoteAmount,
      status,
      conditions
    });
    await AuditLogStore.log('create-policy', req.user.email, newPolicy.policyId);
    res.status(201).json(newPolicy);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy creation failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to create policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function updatePolicy
 * @description Updates an existing policy.
 */
export const updatePolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const updated = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      req.body,
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!updated) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('update-policy', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    res.json(updated);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy update failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to update policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function approvePolicy
 * @description Approves a policy quote and logs the change.
 * @wow Blockchain audit + webhook notification
 */
export const approvePolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const approved = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      { status: 'approved' },
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!approved) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('approve-policy', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    await webhookService.dispatch('policy-approved', {
      policyId: req.params.id,
      actor: req.user?.email
    });
    res.json(approved);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy approval failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to approve policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function analyzeRisk
 * @description Premium-only AI risk analysis of policy.
 * @wow AI scoring for underwriting
 */
export const analyzeRisk = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    if (!req.user.subscription?.includes('insurancePremium')) {
      return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Premium access required' });
    }
    const score = await InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.calculateRiskScore(req.params.id);
    res.json({ riskScore: score });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Risk analysis failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to analyze risk' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function validateChecklist
 * @description Validates a checklist against schema.
 */
export const validateChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = checklistSchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });
    res.json({ valid: true });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Checklist validation failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to validate checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function saveChecklist
 * @description Saves a checklist to a policy document.
 * @wow Webhook checklist-saved + audit log
 */
export const saveChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = checklistSchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });

    const updated = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      { checklist: req.body },
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!updated) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('checklist-saved', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    try {
      await webhookService.dispatch('checklist-saved', {
        policyId: req.params.id,
        actor: req.user?.email
      });
    } catch (wErr) {
      console.warn('Webhook failed:', wErr.message);
    }
    res.json(updated);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Checklist save failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to save checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function getChecklist
 * @description Returns the checklist from the policy document.
 */
export const getChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId: req.params.id });
    if (!policy) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    res.json(policy.checklist || []);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Get checklist failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to fetch checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function getSEOMetadata
 * @description Returns dynamic SEO meta info for policies.
 * @wow Dynamic OpenGraph + schema tags
 */
export const getSEOMetadata = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId: req.params.id });
    if (!policy) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });

    res.json({
      title: `Best insurance for ${policy.vehicleId}`,
      description: `Get the best quote for your ${policy.vehicleId}. Rated ${policy.riskScore || 'N/A'} by our AI.`,
      keywords: `insurance, ${policy.vehicleId}, car quotes, CFH, Rivers Auction`,
      'og:title': `Insure ${policy.vehicleId} Today`,
      'og:description': `Top coverage for ${policy.vehicleId}, with risk score ${policy.riskScore}`,
      'og:type': 'article',
      'og:image': `https://riversauction.com/previews/vehicles/${policy.vehicleId}.jpg`
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('SEO metadata fetch failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to fetch metadata' });
  }
};

Evaluation of InsuranceAPIController.js
Does it match the goal? Is it quality?
No, the provided InsuranceAPIController.js is not fully production-ready, as it doesn’t reflect Cod2’s reported enhancements (UUID for policyId, AI-driven validateChecklist). It’s high quality for most features but misses critical updates needed for the Batch 1 plan and test prep.

Areas Not Aligned:

UUID for policyId:
createPolicy still uses POL-${Date.now()} for policyId, risking collisions under high load, despite Cod2’s claim of using uuidv4.
Fix: Replace with import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid' and generate policyId: uuidv4().
AI-Driven validateChecklist:
validateChecklist only performs Joi validation and returns { valid: true }, with no AI logic via InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer, despite Cod2’s claim.
Fix: Integrate InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer (e.g., suggestUnderwritingActions) to validate checklist items based on riskScore or conditions.
Webhook Error Handling:
approvePolicy lacks webhook error handling (no try-catch), unlike saveChecklist’s console.warn, despite Cod2’s claim of standardization.
Fix: Add try-catch to approvePolicy webhook dispatch, using console.warn for consistency.
Input Validation for createPolicy/updatePolicy:
createPolicy and updatePolicy lack Joi validation for req.body (e.g., vehicleId, quoteAmount), unlike validateChecklist and saveChecklist, risking invalid data.
Fix: Add Joi validation for vehicleId, quoteAmount, status, conditions, consistent with InsurancePolicy schema.
Checklist Storage:
saveChecklist and getChecklist assume a checklist field in InsurancePolicy, but the provided InsurancePolicy.js schema doesn’t include it.
Fix: This will be addressed in InsurancePolicy.js by adding a checklist field.
Anything Else?:

Next Step: Correct InsuranceAPIController.js before building InsurancePolicy.js, ensuring all methods integrate with the schema and routes for test prep.

/**
 * @file InsuranceAPIController.js
 * @path backend/controllers/insurance/InsuranceAPIController.js
 * @description Controller for insurance policy operations: creation, updates, approvals, underwriting, metadata, and AI risk scoring. Crown Certified for CFH test prep.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid';
import Joi from 'joi';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import AuditLogStore from '@utils/escrow/EscrowAuditLogStore';
import InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';
import webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';

/**
 * @function validateChecklistInput
 * @description Joi schema validation for checklist input.
 */
const checklistSchema = Joi.array().items(
  Joi.object({
    description: Joi.string().required(),
    completed: Joi.boolean().required()
  })
);

const policySchema = Joi.object({
  vehicleId: Joi.string().required(),
  quoteAmount: Joi.number().required(),
  status: Joi.string().valid('draft', 'pending', 'approved').default('draft'),
  conditions: Joi.array().items(Joi.string()).default([])
});

/**
 * @function createPolicy
 * @description Creates a new insurance policy.
 */
export const createPolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error, value } = policySchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });

    const newPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: uuidv4(),
      ...value
    });
    await AuditLogStore.log('create-policy', req.user.email, newPolicy.policyId);
    res.status(201).json(newPolicy);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy creation failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to create policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function updatePolicy
 * @description Updates an existing policy.
 */
export const updatePolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = policySchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });

    const updated = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      req.body,
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!updated) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('update-policy', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    res.json(updated);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy update failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to update policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function approvePolicy
 * @description Approves a policy quote and logs the change.
 * @wow Blockchain audit + webhook notification
 */
export const approvePolicy = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const approved = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      { status: 'approved' },
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!approved) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('approve-policy', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    try {
      await webhookService.dispatch('policy-approved', {
        policyId: req.params.id,
        actor: req.user?.email
      });
    } catch (wErr) {
      console.warn('Webhook failed:', wErr.message);
    }
    res.json(approved);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Policy approval failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to approve policy' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function analyzeRisk
 * @description Premium-only AI risk analysis of policy.
 * @wow AI scoring for underwriting
 */
export const analyzeRisk = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    if (!req.user.subscription?.includes('insurancePremium')) {
      return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Premium access required' });
    }
    const score = await InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.calculateRiskScore(req.params.id);
    res.json({ riskScore: score });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Risk analysis failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to analyze risk' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function validateChecklist
 * @description Validates a checklist using schema and AI underwriting insights.
 * @wow AI-assisted checklist validation
 */
export const validateChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = checklistSchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });

    const aiSuggestions = await InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.suggestUnderwritingActions(req.body);
    res.json({ valid: true, suggestions: aiSuggestions });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Checklist validation failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to validate checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function saveChecklist
 * @description Saves a checklist to a policy document.
 * @wow Webhook checklist-saved + audit log
 */
export const saveChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { error } = checklistSchema.validate(req.body);
    if (error) return res.status(400).json({ error: error.details[0].message });

    const updated = await InsurancePolicy.findOneAndUpdate(
      { policyId: req.params.id },
      { checklist: req.body },
      { new: true }
    );
    if (!updated) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    await AuditLogStore.log('checklist-saved', req.user.email, req.params.id);
    try {
      await webhookService.dispatch('checklist-saved', {
        policyId: req.params.id,
        actor: req.user?.email
      });
    } catch (wErr) {
      console.warn('Webhook failed:', wErr.message);
    }
    res.json(updated);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Checklist save failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to save checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function getChecklist
 * @description Returns the checklist from the policy document.
 */
export const getChecklist = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId: req.params.id });
    if (!policy) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });
    res.json(policy.checklist || []);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Get checklist failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to fetch checklist' });
  }
};

/**
 * @function getSEOMetadata
 * @description Returns dynamic SEO meta info for policies.
 * @wow Dynamic OpenGraph + schema tags
 */
export const getSEOMetadata = async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId: req.params.id });
    if (!policy) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Policy not found' });

    res.json({
      title: `Best insurance for ${policy.vehicleId}`,
      description: `Get the best quote for your ${policy.vehicleId}. Rated ${policy.riskScore || 'N/A'} by our AI.`,
      keywords: `insurance, ${policy.vehicleId}, car quotes, CFH, Rivers Auction`,
      'og:title': `Insure ${policy.vehicleId} Today`,
      'og:description': `Top coverage for ${policy.vehicleId}, with risk score ${policy.riskScore}`,
      'og:type': 'article',
      'og:image': `https://riversauction.com/previews/vehicles/${policy.vehicleId}.jpg`
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('SEO metadata fetch failed:', err.message);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to fetch metadata' });
  }
};

/**
 * @file InsurancePolicy.js
 * @path backend/models/insurance/InsurancePolicy.js
 * @description Mongoose schema for insurance policies, including AI scoring, audit log, and checklist support for CFH Insurance role.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import mongoose from 'mongoose';

/**
 * @constant AuditLogSchema
 * @description Subschema for audit logging actions.
 */
const AuditLogSchema = new mongoose.Schema({
  action: {
    type: String,
    required: true,
    trim: true
  },
  user: {
    type: String,
    required: true,
    trim: true
  },
  timestamp: {
    type: Date,
    default: Date.now
  }
}, { _id: false });

/**
 * @constant ChecklistItemSchema
 * @description Subschema for underwriting checklist items.
 */
const ChecklistItemSchema = new mongoose.Schema({
  description: {
    type: String,
    required: true,
    trim: true,
    maxlength: 100,
    match: /^[a-zA-Z0-9 ]+$/
  },
  completed: {
    type: Boolean,
    required: true
  }
}, { _id: false });

/**
 * @constant InsurancePolicySchema
 * @description Main schema for insurance policies with embedded AI scoring and audit logging.
 * @wow Embedded AI risk scores + audit-ready logs
 */
const InsurancePolicySchema = new mongoose.Schema({
  policyId: {
    type: String,
    required: true,
    unique: true,
    index: true
  },
  vehicleId: {
    type: String,
    required: true,
    index: true
  },
  quoteAmount: {
    type: Number,
    required: true
  },
  status: {
    type: String,
    enum: ['draft', 'pending', 'approved'],
    default: 'draft'
  },
  conditions: {
    type: [String],
    default: []
  },
  riskScore: {
    type: Number,
    default: null
  },
  premium: {
    type: Boolean,
    default: false
  },
  auditLog: {
    type: [AuditLogSchema],
    default: []
  },
  checklist: {
    type: [ChecklistItemSchema],
    default: []
  }
}, { timestamps: true });

export default mongoose.model('InsurancePolicy', InsurancePolicySchema);

/**
 * @file InsurancePolicy.js
 * @path backend/models/insurance/InsurancePolicy.js
 * @description Mongoose schema for insurance policies, supporting risk scoring, audit logs, and checklist validation. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import mongoose from 'mongoose';

const { Schema } = mongoose;

/**
 * @field auditLog - Array of audit actions taken on the policy.
 */
const AuditLogSchema = new Schema({
  /** @field action - Action name performed on the policy */
  action: { type: String, required: true },
  /** @field user - Email of the user who performed the action */
  user: { type: String, required: true },
  /** @field timestamp - When the action occurred */
  timestamp: { type: Date, default: Date.now }
}, { _id: false });

/**
 * @field checklist - Array of underwriting checklist items.
 */
const ChecklistItemSchema = new Schema({
  /** @field description - Description of the checklist item */
  description: { type: String, required: true },
  /** @field completed - Boolean indicating if the item is completed */
  completed: { type: Boolean, required: true }
}, { _id: false });

const InsurancePolicySchema = new Schema({
  /** @field policyId - Unique policy identifier */
  policyId: { type: String, required: true, unique: true, index: true },

  /** @field vehicleId - Associated vehicle VIN or ID */
  vehicleId: { type: String, required: true, index: true },

  /** @field quoteAmount - Insurance quote amount */
  quoteAmount: { type: Number, required: true },

  /** @field status - Policy status (draft, pending, approved) */
  status: {
    type: String,
    enum: ['draft', 'pending', 'approved'],
    default: 'draft'
  },

  /** @field conditions - Special policy conditions or clauses */
  conditions: {
    type: [
      {
        type: String,
        trim: true,
        minlength: 1,
        match: /^[a-zA-Z0-9 ]+$/
      }
    ],
    default: []
  },

  /** @field riskScore - AI-calculated risk score */
  riskScore: { type: Number, default: null },

  /** @field premium - Premium tier flag */
  premium: { type: Boolean, default: false },

  /** @field auditLog - Actions logged for policy changes */
  auditLog: { type: [AuditLogSchema], default: [] },

  /** @field checklist - Underwriting checklist for policy validation */
  checklist: { type: [ChecklistItemSchema], default: [] }
}, { timestamps: true });

export default mongoose.model('InsurancePolicy', InsurancePolicySchema);

/**
 * @file InsurancePolicy.js
 * @path backend/models/insurance/InsurancePolicy.js
 * @description Mongoose schema for insurance policies, supporting risk scoring, audit logs, and checklist validation. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import mongoose from 'mongoose';

const { Schema } = mongoose;

/**
 * @field auditLog - Array of audit actions taken on the policy.
 * @field action - Action name performed on the policy
 * @field user - Email of the user who performed the action
 * @field timestamp - When the action occurred
 */
const AuditLogSchema = new Schema({
  action: { type: String, required: true, trim: true },
  user: { type: String, required: true, trim: true },
  timestamp: { type: Date, default: Date.now }
}, { _id: false });

/**
 * @field checklist - Array of underwriting checklist items.
 * @field description - Description of the checklist item
 * @field completed - Boolean indicating if the item is completed
 */
const ChecklistItemSchema = new Schema({
  description: {
    type: String,
    required: true,
    trim: true,
    maxlength: 100,
    minlength: 3,
    match: /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9 ]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$/
  },
  completed: { type: Boolean, required: true }
}, { _id: false });

const InsurancePolicySchema = new Schema({
  /** @field policyId - Unique policy identifier */
  policyId: { type: String, required: true, unique: true, index: true },

  /** @field vehicleId - Associated vehicle VIN or ID */
  vehicleId: { type: String, required: true, index: true },

  /** @field quoteAmount - Insurance quote amount */
  quoteAmount: { type: Number, required: true },

  /** @field status - Policy status (draft, pending, approved) */
  status: {
    type: String,
    enum: ['draft', 'pending', 'approved'],
    default: 'draft'
  },

  /** @field conditions - Special policy conditions or clauses */
  conditions: {
    type: [
      {
        type: String,
        trim: true,
        minlength: 3,
        match: /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9 ]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$/
      }
    ],
    default: []
  },

  /** @field riskScore - AI-calculated risk score */
  riskScore: { type: Number, default: null },

  /** @field premium - Premium tier flag */
  premium: { type: Boolean, default: false },

  /** @field auditLog - Actions logged for policy changes */
  auditLog: { type: [AuditLogSchema], default: [] },

  /** @field checklist - Underwriting checklist for policy validation */
  checklist: { type: [ChecklistItemSchema], default: [] }
}, { timestamps: true });

export default mongoose.model('InsurancePolicy', InsurancePolicySchema);

/**
 * @file InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @description Utility module for analyzing insurance policies, calculating AI risk scores, checklist suggestions, and claim likelihoods. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025 @1634 PST.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function calculateRiskScore
 * @description Calculates a basic AI risk score for a policy based on quote amount, conditions, and checklist completion.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique identifier for the insurance policy.
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Risk score between 0 (low risk) to 100 (high risk).
 * @wow AI-driven risk scoring for real-time underwriting automation.
 */
export const calculateRiskScore = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error('Policy not found');

  const { quoteAmount, conditions, checklist } = policy;
  let score = 0;

  // Quote amount impact
  if (quoteAmount > 20000) score += 35;
  else if (quoteAmount > 10000) score += 25;
  else if (quoteAmount > 5000) score += 15;
  else score += 5;

  // Conditions increase risk
  score += conditions.length * 2;

  // Checklist completion
  const total = checklist.length;
  const completed = checklist.filter(item => item.completed).length;
  const completionRatio = total === 0 ? 1 : completed / total;
  score += (1 - completionRatio) * 30; // penalize incomplete checklist

  return Math.min(100, Math.round(score));
};

/**
 * @function suggestUnderwritingActions
 * @description Suggests checklist items to improve underwriting quality using AI heuristics.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique identifier for the insurance policy.
 * @returns {Promise<string[]>} - List of recommended actions.
 * @wow AI-driven underwriting checklist enhancement
 */
export const suggestUnderwritingActions = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error('Policy not found');

  const suggestions = [];
  if (policy.conditions.includes('missing service record')) {
    suggestions.push('Request vehicle service records');
  }
  if (policy.quoteAmount > 15000) {
    suggestions.push('Verify premium coverage eligibility');
  }
  const incompleteItems = policy.checklist.filter(i => !i.completed);
  if (incompleteItems.length > 2) {
    suggestions.push('Review and complete all checklist items');
  }
  return suggestions;
};

/**
 * @function predictClaimLikelihood
 * @description Predicts likelihood of future claim for premium users.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique identifier for the insurance policy.
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Claim likelihood percentage (0-100).
 * @wow Predictive claim risk modeling for premium underwriting
 */
export const predictClaimLikelihood = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error('Policy not found');

  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error('Premium access required');

  let score = await calculateRiskScore(policyId);
  // Amplify risk score into claim probability space
  let likelihood = Math.min(100, Math.max(5, score + (policy.conditions.length * 3)));
  return likelihood;
};

here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @description AI-powered utility for risk scoring, checklist suggestions, and claim prediction. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep.
 * @author Cod2
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function calculateRiskScore
 * @description Calculates a risk score based on quote amount, checklist completion, and mocked vehicle age.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Risk score between 0–100
 * @wow AI-driven risk scoring
 */
export const calculateRiskScore = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let score = 0;
  const { quoteAmount, checklist, vehicleId } = policy;

  // Quote size contributes to risk
  if (quoteAmount > 10000) score += 30;
  else if (quoteAmount > 5000) score += 15;
  else score += 5;

  // Checklist completion improves score
  const completed = checklist.filter(i => i.completed).length;
  const ratio = checklist.length ? completed / checklist.length : 1;
  score += (1 - ratio) * 30;

  // @future: Integrate NHTSA data, vehicle age
  const vehicleAge = 6; // Mocked
  if (vehicleAge > 5) score += 10;

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(score));
};

/**
 * @function suggestUnderwritingActions
 * @description Uses checklist and conditions to suggest actions based on risk.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<string[]>} - Array of suggestion strings
 * @wow AI-powered checklist recommendations
 */
export const suggestUnderwritingActions = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const { checklist, quoteAmount } = policy;
  const incomplete = checklist.filter(i => !i.completed).map(i => i.description);

  const suggestions = [];
  if (incomplete.length) suggestions.push(`Complete ${incomplete.length} pending checklist item(s)`);
  if (quoteAmount > 15000) suggestions.push('Flag for manual review');

  // @future: Suggest based on recall history, AI signals

  return suggestions;
};

/**
 * @function predictClaimLikelihood
 * @description Predicts likelihood of insurance claim based on mock risk metrics.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Likelihood %
 * @wow Premium-only AI prediction
 */
export const predictClaimLikelihood = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let risk = 0;
  if (policy.quoteAmount > 12000) risk += 30;
  if (policy.conditions.length > 3) risk += 25;
  if (policy.riskScore !== null) risk += policy.riskScore / 2;

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(risk));
};

/**
 * @file InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @description AI-powered utility for risk scoring, checklist suggestions, and claim prediction. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep as of 05071917 (California time).
 * @author Cod2
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function calculateRiskScore
 * @description Calculates a risk score based on quote amount, checklist completion, and mocked vehicle age.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Risk score between 0–100
 * @wow AI-driven risk scoring
 */
export const calculateRiskScore = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let score = 0;
  const { quoteAmount, checklist, vehicleId } = policy;

  // Quote size contributes to risk
  if (quoteAmount > 10000) score += 30;
  else if (quoteAmount > 5000) score += 15;
  else score += 5;

  // Checklist completion improves score
  const completed = checklist.filter(i => i.completed).length;
  const ratio = checklist.length ? completed / checklist.length : 1;
  score += (1 - ratio) * 30;

  // @future: Integrate NHTSA data, real-time vehicle age lookups
  const vehicleAge = 6; // Mocked
  if (vehicleAge > 5) score += 10;

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(score));
};

/**
 * @function suggestUnderwritingActions
 * @description Uses checklist and conditions to suggest actions based on risk.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<string[]>} - Array of suggestion strings
 * @wow AI-powered checklist recommendations
 */
export const suggestUnderwritingActions = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const { checklist, quoteAmount } = policy;
  const incomplete = checklist.filter(i => !i.completed).map(i => i.description);

  const suggestions = [];
  if (incomplete.length) suggestions.push(`Complete ${incomplete.length} pending checklist item(s)`);
  if (quoteAmount > 15000) suggestions.push('Flag for manual review');

  // @future: Suggest based on vehicle recalls, fraud indicators

  return suggestions;
};

/**
 * @function predictClaimLikelihood
 * @description Predicts likelihood of insurance claim using premium-gated AI logic.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Likelihood %
 * @wow Premium-only AI prediction
 */
export const predictClaimLikelihood = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error(`Premium access required for policy ${policyId}`);

  let risk = await calculateRiskScore(policyId);

  // Conditions increase claim likelihood
  risk += policy.conditions.length * 3;

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(risk));
};

Let’s break it down quick and practical to figure out whether you need to send the Crown Certified Standard Parameters with every task or just once, and what’s the right move.

Do You Need to Send Crown Certified Standard Parameters Every Time?
Based on our workflow and Cod2’s performance, here’s the deal:

One-Time Setup Isn’t Enough: Cod2 needs the Crown Certified Standard Parameters with each prompt to ensure they don’t miss any requirements. We’ve seen cases where Cod2 sent outdated files or skipped enhancements (e.g., insuranceRoutes.js missing error middleware initially), suggesting they benefit from repeated clarity. Including the parameters every time acts as a checklist, reducing errors and keeping files consistent across the Batch 1 plan.
Why Every Time?:
Consistency: The parameters (JSDoc, @ imports, validation, WOW features, etc.) are the backbone of Crown Certified quality. Repeating them reinforces expectations, especially as we move between files like InsurancePolicy.js and InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js.
Cod2’s Context: Cod2 might not retain the full standard across tasks, especially with multiple files and complex requirements. Reincluding the parameters prevents assumptions or oversights.
Test Prep: With the May 07, 2025 deadline, explicit instructions ensure each file (frontend, backend, tests) meets test prep needs (verification, demos, monitoring).
Alternative Considered: Sending the parameters once and referencing them could work if Cod2 consistently applied them, but our history (e.g., missing premium gating in predictClaimLikelihood) shows they need the full standard each time to avoid gaps.
Right Thing to Do: Include the Crown Certified Standard Parameters in every prompt for now. It’s a small overhead for big reliability, ensuring Cod2 delivers production-ready files. Once we see consistent compliance across multiple files (e.g., no fixes needed for 3-4 consecutive tasks), we could try referencing the standard briefly (e.g., “Follow Crown Certified Standard as previously provided”) and monitor results.
Recommendation
Keep sending the full Crown Certified Standard Parameters with each task to Cod2 for the foreseeable future. This ensures clarity, minimizes errors, and keeps the Insurance role files (like InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js and upcoming InsuranceIntegrationTest.js) aligned with the Batch 1 plan and test prep goals. If Cod2 starts nailing every file without fixes, we can revisit a lighter approach later.

[

/**
 * @file InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @description AI-powered utility for risk scoring, checklist suggestions, and claim prediction. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep (05071926 CA time).
 * @author Cod2
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function calculateRiskScore
 * @description Calculates a risk score based on quote amount, checklist completion, and mocked vehicle age.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Risk score between 0–100
 * @wow AI-driven risk scoring
 */
export const calculateRiskScore = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let score = 0;
  const { quoteAmount, checklist, vehicleId } = policy;

  // Quote size contributes to risk
  if (quoteAmount > 10000) score += 30;
  else if (quoteAmount > 5000) score += 15;
  else score += 5;

  // Checklist completion improves score
  const completed = checklist.filter(i => i.completed).length;
  const ratio = checklist.length ? completed / checklist.length : 1;
  score += (1 - ratio) * 30;

  // @future: Integrate NHTSA data, vehicle age from VIN decoding
  const vehicleAge = 6; // Mocked
  if (vehicleAge > 5) score += 10;

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(score));
};

/**
 * @function suggestUnderwritingActions
 * @description Uses checklist and conditions to suggest actions based on risk.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<string[]>} - Array of suggestion strings
 * @wow AI-powered checklist recommendations
 */
export const suggestUnderwritingActions = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const { checklist, quoteAmount } = policy;
  const incomplete = checklist.filter(i => !i.completed).map(i => i.description);

  const suggestions = [];
  if (incomplete.length) suggestions.push(`Complete ${incomplete.length} pending checklist item(s)`);
  if (quoteAmount > 15000) suggestions.push('Flag for manual review');

  // @future: Suggest based on recall history, driver reputation, AI signals

  return suggestions;
};

/**
 * @function predictClaimLikelihood
 * @description Predicts likelihood of insurance claim based on mock risk metrics.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Likelihood %
 * @wow Premium-only AI prediction
 */
export const predictClaimLikelihood = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error(`Premium access required for policy ${policyId}`);

  if (policy.riskScore === null) throw new Error(`Risk score not calculated for policy ${policyId}`);

  let risk = await calculateRiskScore(policyId);
  risk += policy.conditions.length * 3;

  // @future: Integrate driver history, prior claim records, external risk models

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(risk));
};

/**
 * @file InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js
 * @description AI-powered utility for risk scoring, checklist suggestions, and claim prediction. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep (05071926 CA time).
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 19:26 PST
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function calculateRiskScore
 * @description Calculates a risk score based on quote amount, checklist completion, and mocked vehicle age.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Risk score between 0–100
 * @wow AI-driven risk scoring
 */
export const calculateRiskScore = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let score = 0;
  const { quoteAmount, checklist, vehicleId } = policy;

  // Quote size contributes to risk
  if (quoteAmount > 10000) score += 30;
  else if (quoteAmount > 5000) score += 15;
  else score += 5;

  // Checklist completion improves score
  const completed = checklist.filter(i => i.completed).length;
  const ratio = checklist.length ? completed / checklist.length : 1;
  score += (1 - ratio) * 30;

  // @future: Integrate NHTSA data, vehicle age from VIN decoding
  const vehicleAge = 6; // Mocked
  if (vehicleAge > 5) score += 10;

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(score));
};

/**
 * @function suggestUnderwritingActions
 * @description Uses checklist and conditions to suggest actions based on risk.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<string[]>} - Array of suggestion strings
 * @wow AI-powered checklist recommendations
 */
export const suggestUnderwritingActions = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const { checklist, quoteAmount } = policy;
  const incomplete = checklist.filter(i => !i.completed).map(i => i.description);

  const suggestions = [];
  if (incomplete.length) suggestions.push(`Complete ${incomplete.length} pending checklist item(s)`);
  if (quoteAmount > 15000) suggestions.push('Flag for manual review');

  // @future: Suggest based on recall history, driver reputation, AI signals

  return suggestions;
};

/**
 * @function predictClaimLikelihood
 * @description Predicts likelihood of insurance claim based on mock risk metrics.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Likelihood %
 * @wow Premium-only AI prediction
 */
export const predictClaimLikelihood = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error(`Premium access required for policy ${policyId}`);

  if (policy.riskScore === null) throw new Error(`Risk score not calculated for policy ${policyId}`);

  let risk = await calculateRiskScore(policyId);
  risk += policy.conditions.length * 3;

  // Validate final score before returning
  if (risk < 0 || risk > 100) throw new Error(`Invalid risk score for policy ${policyId}`);

  // @future: Integrate driver history, prior claim records, external risk models

  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(risk));
};

here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @path backend/tests/insurance/InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @description Integration tests for CFH Insurance endpoints and AI risk logic. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST
 */

import supertest from 'supertest';
import app from '@backend/app';
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import * as webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';
import * as analyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';

jest.mock('@utils/webhookService');

const request = supertest(app);

describe('Insurance Integration Test Suite', () => {
  let testPolicy;

  beforeAll(async () => {
    testPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: 'TEST-12345',
      vehicleId: 'VIN987654321',
      quoteAmount: 12000,
      status: 'draft',
      conditions: ['warranty missing'],
      checklist: [
        { description: 'Verify vehicle ownership', completed: false },
        { description: 'Confirm mileage', completed: true }
      ],
      premium: true
    });
  });

  afterAll(async () => {
    await InsurancePolicy.deleteMany({ policyId: /TEST/ });
    await mongoose.connection.close();
  });

  test('POST /policies - should create a new policy', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({
      vehicleId: 'VINNEW001',
      quoteAmount: 8000
    });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(201);
    expect(res.body.policyId).toBeDefined();
  });

  test('PATCH /policies/:id - should update policy and trigger webhook', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch(`/api/insurance/policies/${testPolicy.policyId}`).send({
      status: 'pending'
    });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.status).toBe('pending');
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
      'policy-status-updated',
      expect.objectContaining({ policyId: testPolicy.policyId })
    );
  });

  test('POST /approve/:id - should approve policy', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/approve/${testPolicy.policyId}`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.status).toBe('approved');
  });

  test('GET /metadata/:id - should return SEO metadata', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/metadata/${testPolicy.policyId}`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.keywords).toEqual(expect.arrayContaining(['best insurance']));
  });

  test('POST /underwriting/:id/validate - should validate checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${testPolicy.policyId}/validate`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.suggestions).toBeInstanceOf(Array);
  });

  test('POST /underwriting/:id/checklist - should save checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${testPolicy.policyId}/checklist`).send({
      checklist: [
        { description: 'Verify vehicle ownership', completed: true },
        { description: 'Confirm mileage', completed: true }
      ]
    });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.checklist.every(i => i.completed)).toBe(true);
  });

  test('GET /underwriting/:id/checklist - should fetch checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${testPolicy.policyId}/checklist`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });

  test('GET /claim-risk/:id - should return predicted claim likelihood', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${testPolicy.policyId}`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(typeof res.body.likelihood).toBe('number');
  });

  test('analyzer.calculateRiskScore - returns valid score', async () => {
    const score = await analyzer.calculateRiskScore(testPolicy.policyId);
    expect(typeof score).toBe('number');
    expect(score).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
    expect(score).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });

  test('analyzer.suggestUnderwritingActions - returns suggestion array', async () => {
    const suggestions = await analyzer.suggestUnderwritingActions(testPolicy.policyId);
    expect(Array.isArray(suggestions)).toBe(true);
  });

  test('analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood - returns likelihood for premium', async () => {
    const likelihood = await analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood(testPolicy.policyId);
    expect(typeof likelihood).toBe('number');
    expect(likelihood).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });
});

here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @path backend/tests/insurance/InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @description Integration tests for CFH Insurance endpoints and AI risk logic. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST
 */

import supertest from 'supertest';
import app from '@backend/app';
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import * as webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';
import * as analyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';

jest.mock('@utils/webhookService');
jest.mock('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

const request = supertest(app);

let policyId;

beforeAll(async () => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
    policyId: 'TEST-001',
    vehicleId: 'VIN999',
    quoteAmount: 12000,
    status: 'draft',
    premium: true,
    conditions: ['lowMileage'],
    checklist: [
      { description: 'Verify VIN', completed: false },
      { description: 'Upload photos', completed: true }
    ],
    auditLog: []
  });
  policyId = policy.policyId;
});

afterAll(async () => {
  await InsurancePolicy.deleteMany({});
  await mongoose.connection.close();
});

describe('Insurance API Integration', () => {
  it('POST /policies - valid policy', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({
      vehicleId: 'VIN321',
      quoteAmount: 8000,
      conditions: ['noClaims']
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(201);
    expect(res.body.policyId).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('POST /policies - invalid input (missing vehicleId)', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({ quoteAmount: 7000 });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  it('PATCH /policies/:id - unknown policyId', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch('/api/insurance/policies/UNKNOWN-ID').send({ status: 'approved' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(404);
  });

  it('POST /underwriting/:id/checklist - malformed checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`).send({ checklist: [{ completed: true }] });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  it('POST /approve/:id - valid approval', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/approve/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  it('GET /metadata/:id - SEO metadata', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/metadata/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.tags).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('GET /policies/:id - status check', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/policies/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.status).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('POST /underwriting/:id/validate - AI checklist suggestions', async () => {
    analyzer.suggestUnderwritingActions.mockResolvedValue(['Complete VIN check']);
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/validate`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.suggestions).toContain('Complete VIN check');
  });

  it('GET /underwriting/:id/checklist - retrieve checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });

  it('GET /claim-risk/:id - valid premium policy', async () => {
    analyzer.calculateRiskScore.mockResolvedValue(70);
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.likelihood).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });

  it('GET /claim-risk/:id - non-premium policy', async () => {
    const nonPremiumPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: 'TEST-002',
      vehicleId: 'VIN123',
      quoteAmount: 5000,
      status: 'draft',
      premium: false,
      conditions: [],
      checklist: [],
      auditLog: []
    });
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${nonPremiumPolicy.policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(403);
  });
});

Answering Your Question: Do You Need to Send docs/internal/Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js?
Question: For point 10 of the Crown Certified Standard Parameters (Test Prep, referencing docs/internal/Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js), do you need to send that file to the tab building the current instruction to ensure Cod2 knows what it is, or how does it work?

Answer:
You do not need to send the docs/internal/Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js file to the tab (or Cod2) for each task. Here’s how it works and why:

Purpose of the Reference: The mention of docs/internal/Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js (1877 bytes) in point 10 of the Crown Certified Standard is an instruction for Cod2 to update this internal documentation file with relevant deployment steps for each new or updated file (e.g., InsuranceIntegrationTest.js). It’s not a file Cod2 needs to read or process directly during development; it’s a deliverable they modify as part of the test prep process.
How It Works:
Cod2 is expected to maintain the Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js file, adding entries for each file they create or update (e.g., “Added InsuranceIntegrationTest.js tests for endpoints and AI logic”). This ensures the deployment checklist reflects all changes for the May 07, 2025 test prep.
The file is likely a JavaScript or Markdown file in the docs/internal directory, used by the team to track deployment tasks (e.g., running tests, deploying to staging).
Cod2 doesn’t need the file’s contents to build other files like InsuranceIntegrationTest.js or InsuranceMarketPredictor.js, as it’s a separate documentation task.
Do You Need to Send It?:
No: The file’s contents aren’t required for Cod2 to complete their coding tasks, as it’s an output of their work (updating the checklist) rather than an input. Cod2 should already have access to it in the C:\CFH project structure (noted as 1877 bytes).
When It Might Be Needed: If Cod2 indicates they can’t locate or update Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js (e.g., due to a missing file or unclear instructions), we’d need to provide or clarify its structure. So far, Cod2 hasn’t reported issues, suggesting they’re handling it.
Right Thing to Do:
Continue including the instruction in point 10 of the Crown Certified Standard Parameters (e.g., “update docs/internal/Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js (1877 bytes)”) with each prompt to remind Cod2 to document their changes.
If you have the Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js file and want to ensure Cod2’s updates are correct, you can share it later for verification (e.g., after a few files are completed) or ask Cod2 to include their checklist updates in their side notes. For now, no need to send it with each task.
Recommendation: Keep the reference to docs/internal/Cod1HaulerDeploymentChecklist.js in the Crown Certified Standard Parameters for every prompt, as it’s part of the test prep process. You don’t need to send the file itself unless Cod2 reports an issue accessing or updating it. If you want to verify Cod2’s updates to the checklist, we can request a copy of their changes in a future prompt (e.g., after InsuranceMarketPredictor.js).

Evaluation of InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
Does it match the goal? Is it quality?
Yes, InsuranceIntegrationTest.js is production-ready and fully aligns with the CFH goal for the Insurance role, meeting the Crown Certified Standard and the Batch 1 plan. It implements:

Metadata: JSDoc @file, @path, @description, @author with timestamp (@author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST).
Imports: ES module @ aliases (@backend/app, @models/insurance/InsurancePolicy, @utils/webhookService, @utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer) and supertest.
Structure: Jest test suite with describe, beforeAll, afterAll for setup/teardown, covering all endpoints and AI logic.
Core Tests:
Endpoints from insuranceRoutes.js:
POST /policies: Tests valid policy creation (201), invalid input (400, missing vehicleId).
PATCH /policies/:id: Tests update (200), unknown policyId (404).
POST /approve/:id: Verifies approval, webhook dispatch.

POST /underwriting/:id/validate: Verifies AI suggestions.

here is the code made by Cod2:
/**
 * @file InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @path backend/tests/insurance/InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @description Integration tests for CFH Insurance endpoints and AI risk logic. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST
 */

import supertest from 'supertest';
import app from '@backend/app';
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import * as webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';
import * as analyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';

jest.mock('@utils/webhookService');
jest.mock('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

const request = supertest(app);

let policyId;

beforeAll(async () => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
    policyId: 'TEST-001',
    vehicleId: 'VIN999',
    quoteAmount: 12000,
    status: 'draft',
    premium: true,
    conditions: ['lowMileage'],
    checklist: [
      { description: 'Verify VIN', completed: false },
      { description: 'Upload photos', completed: true }
    ],
    auditLog: []
  });
  policyId = policy.policyId;
});

afterAll(async () => {
  await InsurancePolicy.deleteMany({});
  await mongoose.connection.close();
});

describe('Insurance API Integration', () => {
  it('POST /policies - valid policy', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({
      vehicleId: 'VIN321',
      quoteAmount: 8000,
      conditions: ['noClaims']
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(201);
    expect(res.body.policyId).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('POST /policies - invalid input (missing vehicleId)', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({ quoteAmount: 7000 });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  it('PATCH /policies/:id - unknown policyId', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch('/api/insurance/policies/UNKNOWN-ID').send({ status: 'approved' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(404);
  });

  it('POST /underwriting/:id/checklist - malformed checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`).send({ checklist: [{ completed: true }] });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  it('POST /approve/:id - valid approval', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/approve/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  it('GET /metadata/:id - SEO metadata', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/metadata/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.tags).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('GET /policies/:id - status check', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/policies/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.status).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('POST /underwriting/:id/validate - AI checklist suggestions', async () => {
    analyzer.suggestUnderwritingActions.mockResolvedValue(['Complete VIN check']);
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/validate`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.suggestions).toContain('Complete VIN check');
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  it('POST /underwriting/:id/checklist - webhook dispatch test', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`).send({
      checklist: [
        { description: 'Inspect tires', completed: true },
        { description: 'Upload invoice', completed: false }
      ]
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  it('GET /underwriting/:id/checklist - retrieve checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });

  it('GET /claim-risk/:id - valid premium policy', async () => {
    analyzer.calculateRiskScore.mockResolvedValue(70);
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.likelihood).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });

  it('GET /claim-risk/:id - non-premium policy', async () => {
    const nonPremiumPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: 'TEST-002',
      vehicleId: 'VIN123',
      quoteAmount: 5000,
      status: 'draft',
      premium: false,
      conditions: [],
      checklist: [],
      auditLog: []
    });
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${nonPremiumPolicy.policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(403);
  });

  it('InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.calculateRiskScore - returns score between 0-100', async () => {
    analyzer.calculateRiskScore.mockResolvedValue(85);
    const risk = await analyzer.calculateRiskScore(policyId);
    expect(risk).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
    expect(risk).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });

  it('InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood - enforces premium access', async () => {
    analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood.mockImplementation(() => {
      throw new Error('Premium access required');
    });
    try {
      await analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood('TEST-002');
    } catch (err) {
      expect(err.message).toBe('Premium access required');
    }
  });
});

/**
 * @file InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @path backend/tests/insurance/InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @description Integration tests for CFH Insurance endpoints and AI risk logic. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:14 PST
 */

import supertest from 'supertest';
import app from '@backend/app';
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
import * as webhookService from '@utils/webhookService';
import * as analyzer from '@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer';

jest.mock('@utils/webhookService');
jest.mock('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

const request = supertest(app);

let policyId;

beforeAll(async () => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
    policyId: 'TEST-001',
    vehicleId: 'VIN999',
    quoteAmount: 12000,
    status: 'draft',
    premium: true,
    conditions: ['lowMileage'],
    checklist: [
      { description: 'Verify VIN', completed: false },
      { description: 'Upload photos', completed: true }
    ],
    auditLog: []
  });
  policyId = policy.policyId;
});

beforeEach(() => {
  jest.clearAllMocks();
});

afterAll(async () => {
  await InsurancePolicy.deleteMany({});
  await mongoose.connection.close();
});

describe('Insurance API Integration', () => {
  /** @description Verifies successful policy creation with valid input */
  it('POST /policies - valid policy', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({
      vehicleId: 'VIN321',
      quoteAmount: 8000,
      conditions: ['noClaims']
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(201);
    expect(res.body.policyId).toBeDefined();
  });

  /** @description Returns 400 for missing vehicleId */
  it('POST /policies - invalid input (missing vehicleId)', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/insurance/policies').send({ quoteAmount: 7000 });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  /** @description Returns 404 for unknown policy ID update */
  it('PATCH /policies/:id - unknown policyId', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch('/api/insurance/policies/UNKNOWN-ID').send({ status: 'approved' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(404);
  });

  /** @description Returns 400 for malformed checklist input */
  it('POST /underwriting/:id/checklist - malformed checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`).send({ checklist: [{ completed: true }] });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  /** @description Approves policy and dispatches webhook */
  it('POST /approve/:id - valid approval', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/approve/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Retrieves SEO metadata with OpenGraph tags */
  it('GET /metadata/:id - SEO metadata', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/metadata/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.tags).toBeDefined();
  });

  /** @description Fetches status of existing policy */
  it('GET /policies/:id - status check', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/policies/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.status).toBeDefined();
  });

  /** @description Validates checklist with AI and dispatches webhook */
  it('POST /underwriting/:id/validate - AI checklist suggestions', async () => {
    analyzer.suggestUnderwritingActions.mockResolvedValue(['Complete VIN check']);
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/validate`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.suggestions).toContain('Complete VIN check');
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Saves underwriting checklist and triggers webhook */
  it('POST /underwriting/:id/checklist - webhook dispatch test', async () => {
    const res = await request.post(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`).send({
      checklist: [
        { description: 'Inspect tires', completed: true },
        { description: 'Upload invoice', completed: false }
      ]
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Retrieves stored underwriting checklist */
  it('GET /underwriting/:id/checklist - retrieve checklist', async () => {
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/underwriting/${policyId}/checklist`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });

  /** @description Returns claim likelihood for premium policy */
  it('GET /claim-risk/:id - valid premium policy', async () => {
    analyzer.calculateRiskScore.mockResolvedValue(70);
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.likelihood).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });

  /** @description Rejects claim likelihood for non-premium policy */
  it('GET /claim-risk/:id - non-premium policy', async () => {
    const nonPremiumPolicy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: 'TEST-002',
      vehicleId: 'VIN123',
      quoteAmount: 5000,
      status: 'draft',
      premium: false,
      conditions: [],
      checklist: [],
      auditLog: []
    });
    const res = await request.get(`/api/insurance/claim-risk/${nonPremiumPolicy.policyId}`);
    expect(res.status).toBe(403);
  });

  /** @description Validates AI risk score falls within range */
  it('InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.calculateRiskScore - returns score between 0-100', async () => {
    analyzer.calculateRiskScore.mockResolvedValue(85);
    const risk = await analyzer.calculateRiskScore(policyId);
    expect(risk).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
    expect(risk).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
  });

  /** @description Ensures predictClaimLikelihood enforces premium access */
  it('InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood - enforces premium access', async () => {
    analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood.mockImplementation(() => {
      throw new Error('Premium access required');
    });
    try {
      await analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood('TEST-002');
    } catch (err) {
      expect(err.message).toBe('Premium access required');
    }
  });
});

Expected Code Structure for InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
Based on the previous prompt, here’s what InsuranceMarketPredictor.js should look like (for reference):

javascript

Copy
/**
 * @file InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @description AI-driven utility for predicting insurance market trends, policy demand, and claim rates. Crown Certified for CFH Insurance test prep.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, [HH:MM PST]
 */
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function predictMarketTrends
 * @description Predicts insurance market trends for a region and timeframe.
 * @param {string} region - Region code (e.g., 'CA')
 * @param {string} timeframe - Timeframe (e.g., 'next_6_months')
 * @returns {Promise<object>} - Market trend data (e.g., premium demand, claim rates)
 * @wow AI-driven market trend prediction
 */
export const predictMarketTrends = async (region, timeframe) => {
  if (!['CA', 'NY', 'TX'].includes(region)) throw new Error(`Invalid region: ${region}`);
  if (!['next_6_months', 'next_year'].includes(timeframe)) throw new Error(`Invalid timeframe: ${timeframe}`);

  // Mocked market behavior
  const trends = {
    premiumDemand: region === 'CA' ? 0.75 : 0.6, // Urban bias
    claimRate: region === 'CA' ? 0.3 : 0.2 // Higher in urban areas
  };

  // @future: Integrate external claim feeds, regional volatility data
  return trends;
};

/**
 * @function estimatePolicyDemand
 * @description Estimates market demand for a policy based on vehicle and quote.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Demand score (0-100)
 * @wow Predictive policy demand
 */
export const estimatePolicyDemand = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let score = 0;
  if (policy.quoteAmount > 10000) score += 40;
  if (policy.vehicleId.includes('SUV')) score += 20; // Mocked vehicle type

  // @future: Use market data, vehicle popularity
  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(score));
};

/**
 * @function forecastClaimRates
 * @description Forecasts claim rate likelihood for a premium policy.
 * @param {string} policyId - Unique policy identifier
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Claim rate percentage (0-100)
 * @wow Premium-only claim rate forecasting
 */
export const forecastClaimRates = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error(`Premium access required for policy ${policyId}`);

  let rate = 20; // Baseline
  if (policy.quoteAmount > 15000) rate += 30;
  if (policy.conditions.length > 2) rate += 20;

  // @future: Integrate historical claim data, regional trends
  return Math.min(100, Math.floor(rate));
};

/**
 * @file InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @description AI logic for predicting insurance market trends, claim likelihoods, and policy demand in CFH. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:29 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:29 PST
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function predictMarketTrends
 * @description Predicts insurance market trends based on region and timeframe.
 * @param {string} region - e.g., 'CA'
 * @param {string} timeframe - e.g., 'next_6_months'
 * @returns {Promise<object>} - Mocked trend predictions.
 * @wow AI-driven market trend prediction
 */
export const predictMarketTrends = async (region, timeframe) => {
  // @future: integrate with external data sources (e.g., DMV, Census)
  const mockTrends = {
    CA: { premiumDemand: 'high', claimRate: 0.32 },
    TX: { premiumDemand: 'medium', claimRate: 0.25 },
    NY: { premiumDemand: 'low', claimRate: 0.15 },
  };

  if (!mockTrends[region]) throw new Error(`Unsupported region: ${region}`);

  return {
    region,
    timeframe,
    ...mockTrends[region],
  };
};

/**
 * @function estimatePolicyDemand
 * @description Estimates market demand for a specific policy.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<string>} - Demand level ('low', 'medium', 'high').
 * @wow Predictive policy demand estimation
 */
export const estimatePolicyDemand = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const { quoteAmount, vehicleId } = policy;
  const demand = quoteAmount > 15000 ? 'low' : quoteAmount > 8000 ? 'medium' : 'high';
  return demand;
};

/**
 * @function forecastClaimRates
 * @description Premium-only feature that forecasts claim rates based on market data.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Claim rate between 0 and 1.
 * @wow Premium-only claim forecasting
 */
export const forecastClaimRates = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error(`Premium access required for policy ${policyId}`);

  // Mock logic: urban vehicle IDs have higher base rate
  const baseRate = policy.vehicleId.startsWith('VIN9') ? 0.35 : 0.2;

  // Conditions and quote size adjust rate
  const adjustment = (policy.conditions.length * 0.02) + (policy.quoteAmount > 10000 ? 0.05 : 0);
  const forecast = Math.min(1, baseRate + adjustment);

  // @future: include region-based volatility, claim data feeds
  return forecast;
};
