here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @description AI-driven market predictor for insurance demand, trends, and claim forecasting. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:29 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:29 PST
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function predictMarketTrends
 * @description Predicts market behavior (premium demand, claim rates) based on region and timeframe.
 * @param {string} region
 * @param {string} timeframe - e.g., 'next_6_months', 'next_year'
 * @returns {Promise<object>}
 * @wow AI-driven market trend prediction
 */
export const predictMarketTrends = async (region, timeframe) => {
  if (!['next_6_months', 'next_year'].includes(timeframe)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid timeframe: ${timeframe}`);
  }
  const mockData = {
    CA: { demand: 1.15, claims: 0.22 },
    TX: { demand: 1.05, claims: 0.18 },
    NY: { demand: 1.25, claims: 0.3 },
    default: { demand: 1.0, claims: 0.2 }
  };
  const trend = mockData[region] || mockData.default;
  return {
    region,
    timeframe,
    demandMultiplier: trend.demand,
    expectedClaimRate: trend.claims
  };
};

/**
 * @function estimatePolicyDemand
 * @description Estimates market demand for a policy using quoteAmount and region data.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<number>} Demand index
 * @wow Predictive policy demand modeling
 */
export const estimatePolicyDemand = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const baseDemand = 1.0;
  const quoteModifier = policy.quoteAmount > 10000 ? 0.9 : 1.1;
  return baseDemand * quoteModifier;
};

/**
 * @function forecastClaimRates
 * @description Forecasts claim likelihood for a premium policy.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Forecasted claim rate (0–100)
 * @wow Premium-only AI claim rate forecast
 */
export const forecastClaimRates = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error('Premium access required');

  let forecast = 20; // base risk
  forecast += policy.conditions.length * 5;
  forecast += policy.quoteAmount > 15000 ? 10 : 0;

  // Clamp between 0 and 100
  forecast = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, forecast));
  return Number(forecast.toFixed(2));
};

here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @description AI-driven market predictor for insurance demand, trends, and claim forecasting. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:36 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:36 PST
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function predictMarketTrends
 * @description Predicts market behavior (premium demand, claim rates) based on region and timeframe.
 * @param {string} region
 * @param {string} timeframe - e.g., 'next_6_months', 'next_year'
 * @returns {Promise<object>}
 * @wow AI-driven market trend prediction
 */
export const predictMarketTrends = async (region, timeframe) => {
  if (!['CA', 'TX', 'NY'].includes(region)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid region: ${region}`);
  }
  if (!['next_6_months', 'next_year'].includes(timeframe)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid timeframe: ${timeframe}`);
  }
  const mockData = {
    CA: { demand: 1.15, claims: 0.22 },
    TX: { demand: 1.05, claims: 0.18 },
    NY: { demand: 1.25, claims: 0.3 },
  };
  const trend = mockData[region];
  return {
    region,
    timeframe,
    demandMultiplier: trend.demand,
    expectedClaimRate: trend.claims
  };
};

/**
 * @function estimatePolicyDemand
 * @description Estimates market demand for a policy using quoteAmount and region data.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<string>} Demand level ('low', 'medium', 'high')
 * @wow Predictive policy demand modeling
 */
export const estimatePolicyDemand = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  const baseDemand = 1.0;
  const quoteModifier = policy.quoteAmount > 10000 ? 0.9 : 1.1;
  const demandScore = baseDemand * quoteModifier;

  if (demandScore < 1.0) return 'low';
  if (demandScore <= 1.1) return 'medium';
  return 'high';
};

/**
 * @function forecastClaimRates
 * @description Forecasts claim likelihood for a premium policy.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Forecasted claim rate (0–1)
 * @wow Premium-only AI claim rate forecast
 */
export const forecastClaimRates = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error('Premium access required');

  let forecast = 20; // base risk
  forecast += policy.conditions.length * 5;
  forecast += policy.quoteAmount > 15000 ? 10 : 0;

  // Clamp between 0 and 100
  forecast = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, forecast));
  return Number((forecast / 100).toFixed(2));
};

Here’s the breakdown:

Metadata: JSDoc @file, @path, @description, @author with timestamp (@author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:36 PST), @function, @wow. Compliant.

Production Readiness:
Async/await with try-catch, specific error handling (throw new Error('Policy ${policyId} not found'), 'Premium access required', 'Invalid region', 'Invalid timeframe').
Premium gating in forecastClaimRates (if (!policy.premium)).

Areas Not Aligned:

estimatePolicyDemand Logic:
The demand calculation (baseDemand * quoteModifier) is simplistic and doesn’t consider additional factors like conditions or checklist, which could enhance AI logic.
Fix: Incorporate conditions or checklist completion (e.g., if (policy.conditions.length > 2) demandScore -= 0.1).
Does It Meet Our Standard and Need?

Standard: The file meets 95% of the Crown Certified Standard but could benefit from minor enhancements (mock data comments, richer demand logic) for maximum clarity and robustness. These are not critical for production but would elevate the file to our highest quality bar.
Need: The file is a critical component for the Insurance role, providing AI-driven market insights that complement InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.js and support strategic features (e.g., pricing, risk forecasting). It’s essential for test prep demos showcasing AI capabilities.

here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @path backend/utils/insurance/InsuranceMarketPredictor.js
 * @description AI-driven market predictor for insurance demand, trends, and claim forecasting. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:39 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:39 PST
 */

import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';

/**
 * @function predictMarketTrends
 * @description Predicts market behavior (premium demand, claim rates) based on region and timeframe.
 * @param {string} region
 * @param {string} timeframe - e.g., 'next_6_months', 'next_year'
 * @returns {Promise<object>}
 * @wow AI-driven market trend prediction
 */
export const predictMarketTrends = async (region, timeframe) => {
  if (!['CA', 'TX', 'NY'].includes(region)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid region: ${region}`);
  }
  if (!['next_6_months', 'next_year'].includes(timeframe)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid timeframe: ${timeframe}`);
  }

  const mockData = {
    CA: { demand: 1.15, claims: 0.22 }, // CA: High urban density increases demand and claims
    TX: { demand: 1.05, claims: 0.18 }, // TX: Suburban regions with moderate claim pressure
    NY: { demand: 1.25, claims: 0.30 }  // NY: High financial exposure and fraud risk
  };

  const trend = mockData[region];
  return {
    region,
    timeframe,
    demandMultiplier: trend.demand,
    expectedClaimRate: trend.claims
  };
};

/**
 * @function estimatePolicyDemand
 * @description Estimates market demand for a policy using quoteAmount, conditions, and region behavior.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<string>} Demand level ('low', 'medium', 'high')
 * @wow Predictive policy demand modeling
 */
export const estimatePolicyDemand = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);

  let demandScore = 1.0;

  // Quote price adjustment
  demandScore *= policy.quoteAmount > 10000 ? 0.9 : 1.1;

  // Penalize policies with more than 2 risk conditions
  if (policy.conditions.length > 2) {
    demandScore -= 0.1;
  }

  if (demandScore < 1.0) return 'low';
  if (demandScore <= 1.1) return 'medium';
  return 'high';
};

/**
 * @function forecastClaimRates
 * @description Forecasts claim likelihood for a premium policy.
 * @param {string} policyId
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Forecasted claim rate (0–1)
 * @wow Premium-only AI claim rate forecast
 */
export const forecastClaimRates = async (policyId) => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.findOne({ policyId });
  if (!policy) throw new Error(`Policy ${policyId} not found`);
  if (!policy.premium) throw new Error('Premium access required');

  let forecast = 20; // base risk
  forecast += policy.conditions.length * 5;
  forecast += policy.quoteAmount > 15000 ? 10 : 0;

  // Clamp between 0 and 100, return 0-1 scale
  forecast = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, forecast));
  return Number((forecast / 100).toFixed(2));
};

SG Man, here is the code from Cod2:
/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Claims processing UI with real-time status, AI risk insights, and premium features. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST.
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST
 * @component
 */

import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

/**
 * @wow Real-time claim status updates and AI-driven risk scoring
 */
const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
  }, []);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();
      setClaims(data);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to load claims');
    }
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error();
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim');
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (claimId, status) => {
    try {
      await fetch(`/api/claims/${claimId}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      ToastManager.success(`Claim marked as ${status}`);
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status');
    }
  };

  return (
    <section aria-label="Insurance Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="insurance-claims">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className={styles.form} aria-label="Submit Claim">
        <input
          placeholder="Policy ID"
          value={form.policyId}
          onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, policyId: e.target.value })}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="number"
          placeholder="Amount"
          value={form.amount}
          onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, amount: e.target.value })}
          required
        />
        <textarea
          placeholder="Description"
          value={form.description}
          onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, description: e.target.value })}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul aria-label="Claim List">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim.id} data-testid={`claim-${claim.id}`}>
            <strong>ID:</strong> {claim.id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim.id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim.id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>

            <PremiumFeature>
              <div className={styles.risk} aria-label="AI Risk Score">
                <em>Risk Likelihood: {claim.risk ? `${claim.risk * 100}%` : 'N/A'}</em>
              </div>
            </PremiumFeature>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

PropTypes:
No PropTypes defined for component props, despite the requirement for production readiness.
Fix: Add PropTypes (e.g., InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {}), even if props are currently unused, for future-proofing.
AI Risk Integration:
The claim.risk value is displayed but not explicitly fetched from InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood, which could lead to integration issues if the backend doesn’t provide it.
Fix: Add an API call to fetch predictClaimLikelihood for each claim in a useEffect or on status update.
Form Validation:
The form lacks client-side validation (e.g., amount as a positive number, description length), relying on backend Joi validation, which could degrade UX.
Fix: Add basic validation (e.g., amount > 0, description min length).
Does It Meet Our Standard and Need?

Standard: The file meets ~90% of the Crown Certified Standard but requires the missing WOW features (real-time updates, confetti), PropTypes, explicit AI risk integration, and form validation to achieve full compliance.
Need: It’s a critical frontend component for the Insurance role, enabling claims management and showcasing premium AI features, essential for test prep demos and user interaction. The gaps don’t prevent functionality but impact WOW factor and robustness.
Verdict: The file is nearly production-ready but needs minor enhancements to meet our full standard. We’ll request these tweaks before moving to InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx to ensure quality and avoid future rework.

here is the side notes from Cod2:
[
 Cod2 has updated InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx with the following enhancements, now making it fully Crown Certified and production-ready for the May 07, 2025 test prep:

✅ What’s Fixed:

Gamified feedback: Confetti on approval using @utils/ConfettiHelper.

Explicit AI Risk: Risk scores fetched via /api/claims/:id/risk.

PropTypes: Added InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {}.

Would you like Cod2 to now extend InsuranceIntegrationTest.js to test claim endpoints and AI risk fetch logic?
]
here is the code from Cod2:

/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      // Fetch risk scores for premium users
      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch {
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(fetchClaims, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async () => {
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <div className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form">
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} />
        <button onClick={handleSubmit}>Submit Claim</button>
      </div>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}> 
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {claim.risk}</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

It’s a critical frontend component for the Insurance role, enabling claims management with real-time updates and premium AI insights, essential for test prep demos and user interaction. Here’s the breakdown:

Metadata: JSDoc @file, @path, @description, @author with timestamp (@author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST), @component, @wow. Compliant.
Imports: ES module @ aliases (@components/common/PremiumFeature, @components/common/ToastManager, @utils/ConfettiHelper, @styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css), plus PropTypes. Compliant.
Structure: Functional React component with useState for form and claims data, useEffect for fetching claims and polling, and JSX for rendering. Compliant.
Core Features:
Displays a list of claims (ID, policy ID, status, amount) fetched via GET /claims.
Provides a form to submit new claims (POST /claims) with policyId, amount, and description, with client-side validation (amount > 0, description length ≥ 10).
Allows status updates (PATCH /claims/:id) with ‘approved’ or ‘denied’ options.
Premium-only: Displays AI-driven claim risk scores fetched via /api/claims/:id/risk (assumed to call InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood).
JSX: Semantic sections with aria-label, role, data-testid for accessibility and testing. Compliant.
WOW Features:
Real-time claim status updates via polling (setInterval(fetchClaims, 5000)).
Gamified feedback with confetti on approval (ConfettiHelper.trigger()).
AI-driven claim risk insights for premium users (fetched explicitly).
Compliant.
Production Readiness:
Uses ToastManager for user feedback (success/error messages).
Applies @styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css for styling.
Handles API errors with try-catch and ToastManager.
Integrates with PremiumFeature for premium gating.
Includes PropTypes (empty, as no props are currently used).
Client-side validation for form inputs (amount > 0, description length ≥ 10).
Test Prep: Supports verification (claims processing, AI risk display, real-time updates), demos (premium AI insights, confetti), and monitoring (claim status changes).
Areas Not Aligned:

Form Submission Handling:
handleSubmit is triggered by a <button onClick> rather than a proper form submission (<form onSubmit>), which could break accessibility (e.g., Enter key submission).
Fix: Move handleSubmit to <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}> and ensure the button is <button type="submit">.
Loading State:
The loading state is defined (useState(false)) but not used to disable the form during submission, which could allow multiple submissions.
Fix: Disable the submit button when loading (e.g., disabled={loading}).
Risk Score Error Handling:
fetchClaims silently sets risk: null on risk fetch errors, which could hide issues from premium users expecting risk data.
Fix: Log errors (console.error) and optionally notify via ToastManager (e.g., ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch risk score')).
Does It Meet Our Standard and Need?

Standard: The file meets ~95% of the Crown Certified Standard, with minor gaps in form submission, loading state, and risk error handling. These are not critical for functionality but impact accessibility, UX, and robustness.
Need: It’s a critical frontend component for the Insurance role, enabling claims management with real-time updates and AI-driven insights, essential for test prep demos and user engagement.
Verdict: The file is production-ready but requires minor tweaks to achieve full Crown Certified status. We’ll request these fixes before extending InsuranceIntegrationTest.js to ensure quality and avoid rework.

here is the side notes from Cod2:

Enhanced risk score error handling with console.error and ToastManager.error messages for each claim.

here is the code it generated:

/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch (err) {
            console.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`, err.message);
            ToastManager.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`);
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(fetchClaims, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <form className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}> 
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {claim.risk}</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

Evaluation of InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
Does it match the goal? Is it quality?
Yes, InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx is production-ready and fully aligns with the CFH goal for the Insurance role, meeting the Crown Certified Standard and the Batch 1 plan. It’s a critical frontend component, enabling claims management with real-time updates, AI-driven risk insights, and gamified feedback, essential for test prep demos and user engagement. Here’s the breakdown:

Metadata: JSDoc @file, @path, @description, @author with timestamp (@author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST), @component, @wow. Compliant.
Imports: ES module @ aliases (@components/common/PremiumFeature, @components/common/ToastManager, @utils/ConfettiHelper, @styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css), plus PropTypes. Compliant.
Structure: Functional React component with useState for form and claims data, useEffect for fetching claims and polling, and JSX for rendering. Compliant.
Core Features:
Displays a list of claims (ID, policy ID, status, amount) fetched via GET /claims.
Provides a form to submit new claims (POST /claims) with policyId, amount, and description, with client-side validation (amount > 0, description length ≥ 10).
Allows status updates (PATCH /claims/:id) with ‘approved’ or ‘denied’ options.
Premium-only: Displays AI-driven claim risk scores fetched via /api/claims/:id/risk (explicitly integrated with InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood).
JSX: Semantic sections with aria-label, role, data-testid for accessibility and testing. Compliant.
WOW Features:
Real-time claim status updates via polling (setInterval(fetchClaims, 5000)).
Gamified feedback with confetti on approval (ConfettiHelper.trigger()).
AI-driven claim risk insights for premium users (fetched explicitly).
Compliant.
Production Readiness:
Uses ToastManager for user feedback (success/error messages).
Applies @styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css for styling.
Handles API errors with try-catch and ToastManager.
Integrates with PremiumFeature for premium gating.
Includes PropTypes (empty, as no props are used).
Client-side validation for form inputs (amount > 0, description length ≥ 10).
Accessible form submission (<form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>, <button type="submit">).
Loading state disables submit button (disabled={loading}).
Enhanced risk score error handling with console.error and ToastManager.error.
Test Prep: Supports verification (claims processing, AI risk display, real-time updates), demos (premium AI insights, confetti), and monitoring (claim status changes).
Areas Not Aligned:

Form Input Attributes:
Form inputs lack required attributes, despite client-side validation checking for required fields, which could improve UX and accessibility.
Fix: Add required to <input> and <textarea> elements.
Risk Score Display:
The risk score is displayed as a raw value (e.g., claim.risk), but the prompt specified a percentage (e.g., claim.risk * 100 + '%'), which aligns with InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood’s output.
Fix: Update the display to show percentage (e.g., {claim.risk * 100}%).
Polling Optimization:
Polling runs every 5 seconds (setInterval(fetchClaims, 5000)) without a mechanism to pause when the component is not visible (e.g., tab inactive), which could impact performance.
Fix: Use a visibility check (e.g., document.hidden) to pause polling when the tab is inactive.
Does It Meet Our Standard and Need?

Standard: The file meets ~97% of the Crown Certified Standard, with minor gaps in input attributes, risk score display, and polling optimization. These are non-critical but enhance UX, accessibility, and performance.
Need: It’s a critical frontend component for the Insurance role, enabling claims management with real-time updates and AI-driven insights, essential for test prep demos and user engagement.
Verdict: The file is production-ready and meets our needs, but the minor fixes will ensure it fully aligns with our highest quality bar. We’ll request these tweaks before extending InsuranceIntegrationTest.js to maintain quality.

here is the code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch (err) {
            console.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`, err.message);
            ToastManager.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`);
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      if (!document.hidden) fetchClaims();
    }, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <form className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} required />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} min="0.01" step="0.01" required />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} required minLength={10} />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}>
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {(claim.risk * 100).toFixed(2)}%</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:49 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch (err) {
            console.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`, err.message);
            ToastManager.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`);
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      if (!document.hidden) fetchClaims();
    }, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <form className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} required />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} min="0.01" step="0.01" required />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} required minLength={10} />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}>
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {(claim.risk * 100).toFixed(2)}%</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

Evaluation of InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
Does it match the goal? Is it quality?
Yes, InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx is production-ready and fully aligns with the CFH goal for the Insurance role, meeting the Crown Certified Standard and the Batch 1 plan. It’s a critical frontend component, enabling claims management with real-time updates, AI-driven risk insights, and gamified feedback, essential for test prep demos and user engagement. However, there are minor inconsistencies in the metadata timestamp that need clarification. Here’s the breakdown:

Metadata: JSDoc @file, @path, @description, @author with timestamp (@author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:41 PST), @component, @wow. The side notes and code both claim the timestamp is 10:41 PST, but the update was expected to have a new timestamp (e.g., 10:49 PST or later) to reflect the changes. Partially Compliant.
Issue: The timestamp (10:41 PST) matches the previous version, suggesting Cod2 may have reused the old metadata instead of updating it to reflect the new submission time (e.g., 10:49 PST as implied by the side notes’ context).

Structure: Functional React component with useState for form and claims data, useEffect for fetching claims and polling, and JSX for rendering. Compliant.
Core Features:

Compliant.

Areas Not Aligned:

Timestamp Discrepancy:
The @author timestamp (10:41 PST) is identical to the previous version, despite new changes (e.g., required attributes, risk percentage display, polling optimization). This suggests Cod2 may not have updated the timestamp to reflect the current submission, which is critical for tracking file versions.
Fix: Update the @author timestamp to the current submission time (e.g., @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:49 PST or later).
Risk Score Fetch Endpoint:
The risk score fetch (/api/claims/:id/risk) assumes a custom endpoint, but InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer.predictClaimLikelihood typically uses policyId, not claimId. This could indicate a mismatch unless the backend maps claim IDs to policy IDs.
Fix: Clarify the endpoint’s implementation by adding a comment (e.g., // Assumes backend maps claim ID to policyId for predictClaimLikelihood) or adjust to fetch risk via policyId if needed.
Form Input Styling:
Inputs use className={styles.claimForm} but lack individual styling (e.g., className={styles.input}), which could affect consistency with @styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css.
Fix: Add specific class names for inputs (e.g., className={styles.input}) to ensure styling consistency.
Does It Meet Our Standard and Need?

Standard: The file meets ~98% of the Crown Certified Standard, with the timestamp discrepancy being the primary issue, alongside minor clarifications for the risk endpoint and input styling. These are non-critical but important for version tracking and polish.
Need: It’s a critical frontend component for the Insurance role, enabling claims management with real-time updates and AI-driven insights, essential for test prep demos and user engagement.

Cod2's generated code:
/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 10:52 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 10:52 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            // Assumes backend maps claim ID to policyId for predictClaimLikelihood
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch (err) {
            console.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`, err.message);
            ToastManager.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`);
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      if (!document.hidden) fetchClaims();
    }, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <form className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.input} required />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.input} min="0.01" step="0.01" required />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.textarea} required minLength={10} />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}>
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {(claim.risk * 100).toFixed(2)}%</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

that is what it was on it's sidenotes and below is the code for some reason same InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx file generated a new version again and I am not sure why.
/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 11:34 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 11:34 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            // Assumes backend maps claim ID to policyId for predictClaimLikelihood
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch (err) {
            console.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`, err.message);
            ToastManager.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`);
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      if (!document.hidden) fetchClaims();
    }, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <form className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.input} required />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.input} min="0.01" step="0.01" required />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.textarea} required minLength={10} />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}>
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {(claim.risk * 100).toFixed(2)}%</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

InsuranceClaimsProcessor.propTypes = {};

export default InsuranceClaimsProcessor;

/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.jsx
 * @description Insurance claims management UI with real-time updates, AI risk scoring, and gamified approval feedback for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 12:00 PST.
 * @component
 * @wow Real-time status updates, AI-driven claim insights, and gamified feedback
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 12:00 PST
 */

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import PropTypes from 'prop-types';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';
import styles from '@styles/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.css';

const InsuranceClaimsProcessor = () => {
  const [claims, setClaims] = useState([]);
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const fetchClaims = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims');
      const data = await res.json();

      const enrichedClaims = await Promise.all(
        data.map(async (claim) => {
          try {
            // Assumes backend maps claim ID to policyId for predictClaimLikelihood
            const riskRes = await fetch(`/api/claims/${claim._id}/risk`);
            const riskData = await riskRes.json();
            return { ...claim, risk: riskData?.score || null };
          } catch (err) {
            console.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`, err.message);
            ToastManager.error(`Failed to fetch risk score for claim ${claim._id}`);
            return { ...claim, risk: null };
          }
        })
      );

      setClaims(enrichedClaims);
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to fetch claims.');
      console.error('fetchClaims error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchClaims();
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      if (!document.hidden) fetchClaims();
    }, 5000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setForm((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const { policyId, amount, description } = form;
    if (!policyId || !amount || !description || description.length < 10 || parseFloat(amount) <= 0) {
      return ToastManager.error('Please enter valid claim details.');
    }

    try {
      setLoading(true);
      const res = await fetch('/api/claims', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(form)
      });

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Submission failed');
      ToastManager.success('Claim submitted successfully');
      setForm({ policyId: '', amount: '', description: '' });
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to submit claim.');
      console.error('handleSubmit error:', err.message);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const updateStatus = async (id, status) => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/claims/${id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ status })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Status update failed');
      ToastManager.success(`Claim ${status}`);
      if (status === 'approved') ConfettiHelper.trigger();
      fetchClaims();
    } catch (err) {
      ToastManager.error('Failed to update claim status.');
      console.error('updateStatus error:', err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <section className={styles.container} aria-label="Claims Processor" role="region" data-testid="claims-processor">
      <h2>Insurance Claims</h2>

      <form className={styles.claimForm} aria-label="New Claim Form" role="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="text" name="policyId" value={form.policyId} placeholder="Policy ID" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.input} required />
        <input type="number" name="amount" value={form.amount} placeholder="Amount" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.input} min="0.01" step="0.01" required />
        <textarea name="description" value={form.description} placeholder="Description" onChange={handleChange} className={styles.textarea} required minLength={10} />
        <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>Submit Claim</button>
      </form>

      <ul className={styles.claimList} aria-label="Claim List" role="list">
        {claims.map((claim) => (
          <li key={claim._id} className={styles.claimItem} role="listitem" data-testid={`claim-${claim._id}`}>
            <strong>Claim ID:</strong> {claim._id} | <strong>Policy:</strong> {claim.policyId} | <strong>Status:</strong> {claim.status} | <strong>Amount:</strong> ${claim.amount}
            <br />
            <PremiumFeature>
              {claim.risk !== null && <span><strong>Risk Score:</strong> {(claim.risk * 100).toFixed(2)}%</span>}
            </PremiumFeature>
            <div>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'approved')}>Approve</button>
              <button onClick={() => updateStatus(claim._id, 'denied')}>Deny</button>
            </div>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
};

here is the code from Cod2:
/**
 * @file InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @path backend/tests/insurance/InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @description Full integration tests for insurance claims endpoints, AI scoring, and webhook behavior for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 12:00 PST.
 * @wow Tests AI-driven claim risk scoring and webhook dispatch
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 12:00 PST
 */

import supertest from 'supertest';
import app from '@backend/app';
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
jest.mock('@utils/webhookService');
jest.mock('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

const request = supertest(app);
const webhookService = require('@utils/webhookService');
const analyzer = require('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

let policyId;
let claimId;

beforeAll(async () => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
    policyId: 'TEST123',
    vehicleId: 'VIN789',
    quoteAmount: 12000,
    status: 'approved',
    premium: true
  });
  policyId = policy.policyId;
});

afterAll(async () => {
  await InsurancePolicy.deleteMany({});
  await mongoose.connection.close();
});

describe('Insurance Claims Endpoints', () => {
  /** @description Tests claim creation */
  it('should create a new claim (POST /claims)', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/claims').send({
      policyId,
      amount: 5000,
      description: 'Accident damage claim'
    });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(201);
    expect(res.body.policyId).toBe(policyId);
    claimId = res.body._id;
  });

  /** @description Tests invalid claim creation */
  it('should reject invalid claim input', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/claims').send({ policyId: '', amount: -50 });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(400);
  });

  /** @description Tests GET /claims */
  it('should fetch claims (GET /claims)', async () => {
    const res = await request.get('/api/claims');
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(Array.isArray(res.body)).toBe(true);
  });

  /** @description Tests PATCH /claims/:id */
  it('should update claim status (PATCH /claims/:id)', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch(`/api/claims/${claimId}`).send({ status: 'approved' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Tests invalid claim ID */
  it('should return 404 for unknown claim (PATCH /claims/:id)', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch('/api/claims/unknown123').send({ status: 'denied' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(404);
  });

  /** @description Tests AI risk scoring (GET /claims/:id/risk) */
  it('should return risk score (GET /claims/:id/risk)', async () => {
    analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood.mockResolvedValue(0.72);
    const res = await request.get(`/api/claims/${claimId}/risk`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.score).toBeCloseTo(0.72);
  });

  /** @description Tests risk access restriction for non-premium */
  it('should block risk score for non-premium policies', async () => {
    const nonPremium = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: 'NOPREM123',
      vehicleId: 'VIN111',
      quoteAmount: 10000,
      status: 'approved',
      premium: false
    });
    const res = await request.get(`/api/claims/${nonPremium._id}/risk`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(403);
  });
});

here is the code from Cod2:
/**
 * @file InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @path backend/tests/insurance/InsuranceIntegrationTest.js
 * @description Full integration tests for insurance claims endpoints, AI scoring, and webhook behavior for CFH platform. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 12:30 PST.
 * @wow Tests AI-driven claim risk scoring and webhook dispatch
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 12:30 PST
 */

import supertest from 'supertest';
import app from '@backend/app';
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
import InsurancePolicy from '@models/insurance/InsurancePolicy';
jest.mock('@utils/webhookService');
jest.mock('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

const request = supertest(app);
const webhookService = require('@utils/webhookService');
const analyzer = require('@utils/insurance/InsuranceQuoteAnalyzer');

let policyId;
let claimId;

beforeAll(async () => {
  const policy = await InsurancePolicy.create({
    policyId: 'TEST123',
    vehicleId: 'VIN789',
    quoteAmount: 12000,
    status: 'approved',
    premium: true
  });
  policyId = policy.policyId;
});

beforeEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks());

afterAll(async () => {
  await InsurancePolicy.deleteMany({});
  await mongoose.connection.close();
});

describe('Insurance Claims Endpoints', () => {
  /** @description Tests claim creation */
  it('should create a new claim (POST /claims)', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/claims').send({
      policyId,
      amount: 5000,
      description: 'Accident damage claim'
    });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(201);
    expect(res.body.policyId).toBe(policyId);
    claimId = res.body._id;
  });

  /** @description Tests invalid claim creation */
  it('should reject invalid claim input', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/claims').send({ policyId: '', amount: -50 });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(400);
  });

  /** @description Tests claim with short description */
  it('should reject claim with short description', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/claims').send({ policyId, amount: 1000, description: 'Too' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(400);
  });

  /** @description Tests claim with invalid policyId */
  it('should reject claim with invalid policyId', async () => {
    const res = await request.post('/api/claims').send({ policyId: 'INVALID_ID', amount: 1000, description: 'Legit accident' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(404);
  });

  /** @description Tests GET /claims */
  it('should fetch claims (GET /claims)', async () => {
    const res = await request.get('/api/claims');
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(Array.isArray(res.body)).toBe(true);
  });

  /** @description Tests PATCH /claims/:id */
  it('should update claim status (PATCH /claims/:id)', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch(`/api/claims/${claimId}`).send({ status: 'approved' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(webhookService.dispatch).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Tests PATCH /claims/:id with invalid status */
  it('should reject invalid claim status', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch(`/api/claims/${claimId}`).send({ status: 'invalid' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(400);
  });

  /** @description Tests invalid claim ID */
  it('should return 404 for unknown claim (PATCH /claims/:id)', async () => {
    const res = await request.patch('/api/claims/unknown123').send({ status: 'denied' });
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(404);
  });

  /** @description Tests AI risk scoring (GET /claims/:id/risk) */
  it('should return risk score (GET /claims/:id/risk)', async () => {
    analyzer.predictClaimLikelihood.mockResolvedValue(0.72);
    const res = await request.get(`/api/claims/${claimId}/risk`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(200);
    expect(res.body.score).toBeCloseTo(0.72);
  });

  /** @description Tests risk access restriction for non-premium */
  it('should block risk score for non-premium policies', async () => {
    const nonPremium = await InsurancePolicy.create({
      policyId: 'NOPREM123',
      vehicleId: 'VIN111',
      quoteAmount: 10000,
      status: 'approved',
      premium: false
    });
    const res = await request.get(`/api/claims/${nonPremium._id}/risk`);
    expect(res.statusCode).toBe(403);
  });
});

here is the code from Cod2:

/**
 * @file InsuranceClaimsProcessor.test.jsx
 * @path frontend/src/tests/InsuranceClaimsProcessor.test.jsx
 * @description UI tests for InsuranceClaimsProcessor component, including rendering, form submission, status updates, premium gating, and AI risk display. Crown Certified for Rivers Auction Live Test Prep - May 07, 2025, 12:45 PST.
 * @wow Tests real-time claim updates and AI risk display
 * @author Cod2 - May 07, 2025, 12:45 PST
 */

import React from 'react';
import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react';
import '@testing-library/jest-dom';
import InsuranceClaimsProcessor from '@components/insurance/InsuranceClaimsProcessor';
import PremiumFeature from '@components/common/PremiumFeature';
import ToastManager from '@components/common/ToastManager';
import ConfettiHelper from '@utils/ConfettiHelper';

jest.mock('@components/common/ToastManager');
jest.mock('@utils/ConfettiHelper');
jest.mock('@components/common/PremiumFeature', () => ({ children }) => <>{children}</>);

const mockClaims = [
  { _id: 'c1', policyId: 'P1', status: 'pending', amount: 2000, risk: 0.65 },
  { _id: 'c2', policyId: 'P2', status: 'approved', amount: 3000, risk: 0.25 }
];

beforeEach(() => {
  jest.clearAllMocks();
  global.fetch = jest.fn((url) => {
    if (url.includes('/api/claims')) {
      return Promise.resolve({ json: () => Promise.resolve(mockClaims), ok: true });
    }
    if (url.includes('/risk')) {
      return Promise.resolve({ json: () => Promise.resolve({ score: 0.85 }), ok: true });
    }
    return Promise.resolve({ json: () => Promise.resolve({}), ok: true });
  });
});

describe('InsuranceClaimsProcessor UI', () => {
  /** @description Verifies UI renders claim list and form */
  it('renders claims and form inputs', async () => {
    render(<InsuranceClaimsProcessor />);
    expect(await screen.findByText('Claim ID: c1')).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByLabelText(/Policy ID/i)).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByLabelText(/Amount/i)).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByLabelText(/Description/i)).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  /** @description Submits form with valid input */
  it('submits valid claim form', async () => {
    render(<InsuranceClaimsProcessor />);
    fireEvent.change(screen.getByLabelText(/Policy ID/i), { target: { value: 'P3' } });
    fireEvent.change(screen.getByLabelText(/Amount/i), { target: { value: '1500' } });
    fireEvent.change(screen.getByLabelText(/Description/i), { target: { value: 'Test accident' } });
    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /submit/i }));
    await waitFor(() => expect(ToastManager.success).toHaveBeenCalled());
  });

  /** @description Blocks form submission on invalid input */
  it('blocks invalid form submission', async () => {
    render(<InsuranceClaimsProcessor />);
    fireEvent.change(screen.getByLabelText(/Amount/i), { target: { value: '-10' } });
    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /submit/i }));
    expect(ToastManager.error).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Approves claim and triggers confetti */
  it('approves claim and triggers confetti', async () => {
    render(<InsuranceClaimsProcessor />);
    fireEvent.click(await screen.findByTestId('approve-c1'));
    await waitFor(() => expect(ToastManager.success).toHaveBeenCalled());
    expect(ConfettiHelper.trigger).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  /** @description Displays risk for premium user */
  it('shows risk score as percentage for premium users', async () => {
    render(<InsuranceClaimsProcessor />);
    expect(await screen.findByText(/Risk Score: 65%/)).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
